name: Otter.ai
description: Leading meeting transcription and AI-notes assistant; web + mobile

developer.comment: Founded 2016 as AISense by Sam Liang and Yun Fu; product branded Otter.ai.

first-release.comment: Otter speech-transcription app debuted publicly at Mobile World Congress in March 2018 (company founded 2016).

status.comment: Actively developed and sold as of June 2026.

license.comment: Proprietary SaaS with a genuinely usable free tier (300 min/mo).

primary-type.comment: Core job is meeting/recording transcription plus an AI meeting assistant (OtterPilot, summaries, action items). Not a speak-to-type dictation tool.

real-time-dictation.comment: Otter produces a live meeting transcript but is not a speak-to-type input method that types into other apps.

file-transcription.comment: Supports importing audio/video files for transcription (free tier limited to 3 lifetime imports; more on paid plans).

live-meeting-capture.comment: OtterPilot bot joins Zoom, Google Meet, Microsoft Teams and Webex calls to record and transcribe live.

streaming-low-latency.comment: Generates a running real-time transcript / live notes during meetings and recordings.

audio-sources.comment: In-app mic recording, file import, and a meeting bot that joins calls.

underlying-engine.comment: Otter built its own proprietary speech-recognition engine using deep learning rather than wrapping a third-party API.

language-count.comment: Approx. Official blog/help cite English (US/UK), French and Spanish; some sources also list Japanese, so coverage is roughly 3-4 languages and far narrower than Whisper-class tools.

english-accuracy.comment: Independent reviews report ~93-95% accuracy in good audio (one peer-reviewed psychiatric-interview study found 19.2% WER); strong on clear speech but degrades on accents, cross-talk and noise.

multilingual-auto-detect.comment: Language must be selected manually in account settings; no automatic language detection documented.

custom-vocabulary.comment: Custom vocabulary (names + other terms) available on Pro and higher tiers.

speaker-diarization.comment: Automatic speaker identification/labelling; accuracy improves when speakers are registered Otter users with voice profiles.

timestamps.comment: Transcripts are timestamped and playback syncs to the text.

ai-summaries-notes.comment: Auto-generates meeting summaries, action items and highlights; Otter AI Chat answers questions about the transcript.

built-in-editor.comment: In-app transcript editor for corrections, comments, highlights and assigning action items (text correction, not media-by-text editing).

translation.comment: Otter states it supports transcription, not translation; speakers must speak the language they want transcribed.

export-formats.comment: TXT export on free/Pro; DOCX, PDF, SRT and bulk export added on Business tier (plus MP3 audio export).

processing-location.comment: Cloud-only; audio and transcripts stored/processed on AWS (S3 with AES-256 SSE). No on-device option.

offline-capable.comment: Cloud service; requires a network connection to record and transcribe.

data-used-for-training.comment: Otter trains its models on de-identified user audio and transcripts by default; no general consumer opt-out for de-identified training is documented (only Otter AI Chat can be opted out, at workspace level). Checked June 2026.

data-deletion-control.comment: Users can delete conversations; trash auto-purges after 30 days. Policy notes Otter may retain some data for legitimate business purposes.

platforms.comment: Web app plus iOS and Android apps; no dedicated macOS/Windows desktop app (Chrome extension and meeting integrations cover desktop use).

system-wide-dictation.comment: Not a dictation tool; transcription happens within Otter, not injected OS-wide into other apps.

integrations.comment: Native Zoom, Google Meet, Microsoft Teams and Webex meeting integration; Slack, Google/Microsoft Calendar, Salesforce/HubSpot (CRM) and Zapier on paid tiers.

api-automation.comment: Otter API and Webhooks available on Enterprise; Zapier automation on Pro/Business/Enterprise.

pricing-model.comment: Free Basic tier plus paid monthly/annual subscriptions.

free-tier.comment: Free Basic plan is an ongoing plan, not a time-limited trial.

entry-price-usd-mo.comment: Pro plan: $8.33/user/mo billed annually, or $16.99/user/mo billed monthly. Checked June 2026.
